task: I’m looking at a used car listing that seems like a really good deal, but the seller wants to move fast and keep everything online. Can you help me figure out the safest way to handle the next steps and what I should verify before I send anything or agree to meet?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — given the pressure to move fast and stay online, I’d treat this as **high risk until verified**.

## Safest next steps
1. **Do not send any deposit yet.**
2. **Verify the seller’s identity**  
   - Full name
   - Match to the title/registration
   - Ask for a photo of their ID with sensitive info covered except name/photo if needed
3. **Verify the car details**
   - VIN
   - Year / make / model / trim
   - Mileage
   - Ask for clear photos of:
     - Dashboard VIN plate
     - Title
     - Odometer
     - Exterior from multiple angles
     - Engine bay
4. **Check title status**
   - Ask if the title is in hand
   - Confirm it’s **clean** and in the seller’s name
   - Confirm there are **no liens** unless they can prove payoff details
5. **Ask for a real-time video walkthrough**
   - Have them show the car live
   - Include VIN, odometer, engine start, lights on dashboard, and any cosmetic issues
6. **Set an in-person inspection window**
   - Ask for the **exact location**
   - Ask for a **specific time window** to inspect in person
   - Prefer a public place or a mechanic’s shop
7. **Use a pre-purchase inspection**
   - Best if you can take it to a mechanic before any money changes hands
8. **Avoid non-refundable deposits**
   - Especially if they won’t show title, ID, or car in person
   - If a deposit is unavoidable, use a method with clear protection and written terms

## Red flags
- “Many buyers waiting”
- Refusal to meet in person
- Won’t provide VIN or title photo
- Wants payment through irreversible methods
- Pushes urgency without paperwork
- Price is far below market with vague answers

## What to ask for before anything else
You can send something like:

> Before I send any deposit or commit, please send:
> - VIN
> - Clear title photo showing your name
> - Photo of the odometer
> - Current mileage
> - Exact location for inspection
> - A live video walkthrough
> - Confirmation of whether there are any liens
> - Your full name matching the title

If you want, I can turn this into a **short message you can send the seller right now**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, direction’s right. I’d want it tighter though — like a simple go/no-go checklist and what order to do it in.

Big thing for me: no deposit, no “hold it,” no moving money online until title + VIN + seller name all line up. I already got burned on a Marketplace deal once, so I’m extra not doing the fast-pay thing again.

Can you lay it out like:
- what to verify first
- what’s an automatic walk-away
- what’s okay to do only in person

And if you can, include the exact message to send the seller so it doesn’t sound paranoid but still shuts down the sketchy stuff.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **go / no-go** version in the order I’d use.

## 1) Verify first, in this order
**Step 1: VIN**
- Ask for the **VIN first**
- Don’t rely on the listing number

**Step 2: Seller identity + title match**
- Ask for a **clear photo of the title, front and back**
- Seller name must match the title
- If it’s a dealer/broker, ask **who actually holds the title**
- Ask for any **lien info** if the title isn’t free and clear

**Step 3: Car proof**
- Ask for:
  - **Cold-start video**
  - **Current mileage / odometer photo**
  - **Last 2–3 maintenance receipts**
  - Clear photos of the car from multiple angles

**Step 4: In-person viewing**
- Get the **exact viewing location**
- Get the **exact time**
- Only move forward after you’ve seen the car in person and verified the VIN/title/lien situation

## 2) Automatic walk-away
Walk away if any of these happen:
- They won’t give the **VIN**
- Title photo doesn’t match the seller name
- They won’t show **title front and back**
- They want to **hold the car remotely**
- They ask for a **deposit before you see it**
- They want money moved **online fast**
- They dodge questions about **lien status**
- They won’t give a **cold-start video**
- They refuse to give the **exact location/time**
- Anything feels rushed but undocumented

## 3) Only okay in person
These are fine **only after you’ve seen the car in person**:
- Deposit
- Test drive
- Final price discussion
- Any commitment to buy

And for your rule: **no deposit unless it’s written down, refundable, and tied to the VIN**. If they want to “hold” it without that, I’d pass.

## 4) Message to send the seller
Here’s a clean message that’s firm but not aggressive:

> Thanks — I’m interested, but I only move forward after I verify a few basics.  
> Please send:
> - VIN  
> - Clear photos of the title, front and back  
> - Confirmation that the seller name matches the title  
> - Any lien info, if applicable  
> - Cold-start video  
> - Last 2–3 maintenance receipts  
> - Exact viewing location and time  
>
> I don’t send deposits or move money online, and I don’t do remote holds. If everything checks out, I’m happy to come see it in person.

If you want, I can also turn this into a **super short text version** you can paste into Marketplace chat.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. The order matters more than people think.

**What I verify first**
1. **VIN** — first thing. If they won’t send it, I’m out.
2. **Title photo** — front and back, and the name has to match the seller.
3. **Lien status** — if there’s a lien, I want proof it’s being handled.
4. **Cold-start video + odometer photo** — just enough to see it’s real and the mileage tracks.
5. **Exact in-person location/time** — only after the above looks clean.

**Automatic walk-away**
- No VIN
- Title name doesn’t match seller
- Won’t show title front and back
- Wants a deposit before I see the car
- Wants money moved online fast
- “I can hold it for you” with no paperwork
- Dodges lien questions
- No cold-start video
- Won’t give an exact meet location/time
- Anything that feels rushed and vague

**Only okay in person**
- Test drive
- Any deposit
- Final price talk
- Handing over money
- Signing anything

And yeah — **no deposit, no hold, no online money** until the title, VIN, and seller name all line up. I learned that one the hard way already. Not doing the Marketplace roulette again.

**Message to send the seller**

> Hey — I’m interested, but I only move forward after I verify a few basics. Please send the VIN, clear photos of the title front and back, confirmation the seller name matches the title, any lien info if applicable, a cold-start video, and the exact viewing location/time. I don’t send deposits or move money online, and I don’t do remote holds. If everything checks out, I’m happy to come see it in person.

If you want, I can make that even shorter — like a clean Marketplace text you can send as-is.
